barRaise.topFinding: Twenty-ninth review, tenth straight healthy, and the cycle the board's longest-standing action finally closed. The observability MEDIUM that topped the action list for roughly nine reviews is resolved: the in-flight v0.52.1 ships tools/audit_dashboard.py + scripts/sync-audit-to-dashboard.ps1 + scripts/register-sync-audit.ps1 (a daily 02:30 sync-audit-yac-dashboard task) plus a write-dashboard-status.ps1 integration, a deterministic, no-LLM, FUSE-safe refresh shared by both the daily and the release writers. It is not theoretical: the dashboard Chains.json audit block now reads docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-24.md with critical=1, which the old release-welded refresh could not have shown (last commit v0.52.0, 06-22). The new standing risk is the other half of that same hazard: FUSE working-tree truncation recurred a second straight day, this time across three files at once (CONTEXT.md, BACKLOG.md, and the CLAUDE.md handler), each carrying real 0.52.1 edits and a chopped tail. The daily audit caught it and recovery is complete (all three tails now match HEAD), but two days running on a multi-file edit batch is a pattern, prevention on the edit path is still absent, and release.ps1 has no truncation tripwire, so a corrupted-canon commit is one mistimed release away. That is the finding to act on before 1.0.

actions.title: Add a working-tree truncation tripwire to tools/preship.sh and the release.ps1 PS-native path that hard-blocks a release on any canonical-doc tail mismatch vs HEAD, so prevention does not depend on the daily audit firing before release.ps1. Route canonical-doc edits through tools/safe_write.py. FUSE truncation has now recurred two straight days, including the CLAUDE.md handler.
Post-1.0, split the worker/src/index.js 5,510-line monolith into modules (now tracked as BACKLOG Â§2.3.6). No pre-1.0 action.
